content.verdict: Villa Treville is the most exclusive and private hotel in Positano — the former private residence of legendary film director Franco Zeffirelli, with just seven suites set within verdant terraced gardens descending to the sea. Staying here is less like checking into a hotel and more like being loaned a magnificent private home for a week, with a dedicated staff who exist solely to fulfill every whim. At these prices, it is a once-in-a-lifetime splurge for very special honeymoons, and the experience is utterly unlike any other on the Amalfi Coast.
content.best_room: The Villa del Direttore — Zeffirelli's own suite — is the most extraordinary room on the property, with his personal art collection, a private terrace garden, and the most intimate sense of sleeping inside a work of art. Each of the seven suites is radically different; the Zeffirelli Suite and the Torre Suite with its private tower are also exceptional. Whichever suite you book, request the pre-arrival preferences form and personalize your stay in advance.

itinerary_7_nights.title: A Private Kingdom Arrives
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrive by private boat from Naples for the most dramatic entrance in Positano — the Villa's boathouse and private jetty sit directly at the waterline below the terraced gardens. The staff greet you by name and escort you through the bougainvillea-draped pathways to your suite. A chilled bottle of vintage Champagne awaits on your terrace, and the villa's private chef will prepare whatever you desire for your first dinner by candlelight under the stars.

itinerary_7_nights.title: The Gardens & Private Pool
itinerary_7_nights.description: Zeffirelli spent decades curating the villa's extraordinary gardens, and a morning exploring them with no other guests around — just you and the hummingbirds among jasmine and lemon trees — is one of the great private pleasures of the Amalfi Coast. The terraced pool is yours to command, and a private yoga instructor can be arranged for the lower garden at sunrise.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Positano at Dawn
itinerary_7_nights.description: Walk into Positano village before 9am when the lanes are nearly empty and the light is golden. Buy pastries from Mulino a Vento and eat them on the steps of the church with a view over the pastel-colored village tumbling to the sea. Return to Villa Treville for a long morning swim, then order an in-suite lunch prepared by the private chef before an afternoon of reading on your terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private Boat to Li Galli
itinerary_7_nights.description: Hire one of the villa's private boats and spend the day cruising to the Li Galli archipelago — three tiny islands just off the coast rumored to be the home of Homer's Sirens. Anchor in the crystalline water, snorkel over the seagrass meadows, and have the chef prepare a cold seafood lunch on board. Return at sunset with the Amalfi Coast glowing behind you.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Art, Wine & Culture
itinerary_7_nights.description: The villa's wine cellar — a personal collection amassed by Zeffirelli over decades — is part of the property's extraordinary heritage. Ask the butler to arrange a private tasting of Campanian and Italian wines in the cellar at sunset, paired with local charcuterie and cheeses. Before dinner, spend an hour with the villa's art dossier, which documents the provenance of each piece in the collection — most have remarkable stories.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Day Trip to Capri
itinerary_7_nights.description: The private boat can ferry you to Capri in about 45 minutes. Arrange for a private guide on the island to take you to the quieter Villa Jovis (Emperor Tiberius's clifftop palace) rather than the crowded Piazzetta, then lunch at a seafood restaurant in the Marina Piccola bay. Return in the evening for the villa's chef to prepare a romantic last dinner in the villa gardens.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: The final morning is best spent entirely in the garden and pool with no agenda whatsoever. The butler will arrange a boat back to Naples for your departure, but until then this extraordinary private world remains entirely yours. Zeffirelli believed that beauty should be experienced without rush — and a final morning at Villa Treville understands that completely.

caveats: Villa Treville is one of the most expensive hotels in Italy — minimum stays of 3-4 nights are typically required in peak season, and the villa sometimes rents exclusively to a single party. Verify pricing and availability structure carefully.
The villa has seven suites and no formal spa facilities on-site; in-room treatments can be arranged but it is not a wellness resort in the traditional sense.
Access requires navigating the steps and terraces that are intrinsic to Amalfi Coast properties; guests with mobility issues should discuss room placement with the property in advance.

faqs.question: Is Villa Treville good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: For couples where budget is not the primary constraint, Villa Treville is in a category of its own. The combination of extreme privacy, a legendary historical setting, personalized service from a dedicated staff, and the sheer beauty of the gardens and suites creates a honeymoon experience that is simply unrivaled on the Amalfi Coast.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: June and September are ideal. June offers long light-filled days before the August crowds arrive; September brings a beautiful softness to the light and warm seas with far fewer tourists. May is also lovely — the gardens are in full bloom and temperatures are perfect. The villa is closed from November through March.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Book as far in advance as possible — availability is extremely limited with only seven suites. Communicate your honeymoon preferences, dietary requirements, wine interests, and any special experiences you want (private boat, specific excursions) well before arrival so the team can prepare. The more detail you provide in advance, the more bespoke your experience will be.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: Villa Treville does not have a formal adults-only policy, but with only seven suites and a clientele that skews strongly to couples and connoisseurs of private luxury, the experience is entirely adult-focused in practice.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: The Villa del Direttore — Zeffirelli's personal suite — is the most memorable room in the property, surrounded by his art collection and with its own garden terrace. The Zeffirelli Suite and the Torre Suite are also exceptional. All seven suites are genuinely unique; discuss your preferences with the reservation team and they will match you with the right space.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Arriving by private speedboat from Naples is the most spectacular option (about 1 hour) and can be booked through the villa. By land, a private car from Naples airport takes about 1.5-2 hours to Positano. A water taxi from Positano harbor then brings you directly to the villa's private jetty. The villa team handles all arrival logistics seamlessly.
